app.name: lean-ctx
app.one_liner: Hybrid Context Optimizer — Shell Hook + MCP Server that reduces LLM token consumption by 89-99% via caching, compression, and intelligent tooling in a single Rust binary.

screens.name: Dashboard
screens.purpose: Monitor active context optimizations, view cached files, and manage tool configurations.

rust_commands.name: ctx_execute
rust_commands.purpose: Sandboxed code execution in 11 languages with smart truncation and timeout control.

rust_commands.name: ctx_read
rust_commands.purpose: Cached file reading with hash-based deduplication to minimize token usage.

rust_commands.name: handle_redirect
rust_commands.purpose: Intercept and conditionally bypass file reads for binary types (images, PDFs, etc.) to prevent blocking AI image viewing.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add configurable disabled_tools support in config.toml and env var
week 1: Fix remote dashboard auth to allow HTML access while protecting API endpoints
week 1: Implement binary file passthrough in hook redirect logic
acceptance_tests: Binary files (e.g., .png, .pdf) are passed through to native Read tool without interception when lean-ctx hook is active.
ctx_execute runs Python/JS/Rust scripts in isolated subprocesses and returns only stdout with head+tail preservation on large outputs.
Remote browser access to dashboard loads HTML freely and injects bearer token into /api/* calls automatically.
Setting LEAN_CTX_DISABLED_TOOLS excludes specified tools from MCP list, reducing prompt token overhead.
